info.title: Redis Agent Memory Control Plane API reference
info.description:
Admin API for the Redis Agent Memory self-managed Control Plane.

Authentication: every endpoint requires `Authorization: Bearer <admin-token>`. This is distinct from the agent keys used by the Data Plane. Operational endpoints (health, build-info) are served separately and are unauthenticated.

Self-managed deployments use a shared store database. Embedding configuration is resolved server-side from deployment config rather than supplied in Control Plane requests.

get.description: List agent-key metadata. Secret values are never returned.

post.description: Mint a new agent key. Every grant must reference an existing resource. The token is returned only in this response.

delete.description: Revoke an agent key: deleting the record revokes the key (effective at the data plane within its cache interval).

parameters.name: keyId
parameters.description: Agent-key id: a UUID as 32 lowercase hex chars (the embedded, non-secret part of the bearer).

schema.description: Agent-key id: a UUID as 32 lowercase hex chars (the embedded, non-secret part of the bearer).

parameters.description: Agent-key id: a UUID as 32 lowercase hex chars (the embedded, non-secret part of the bearer).

schema.description: Agent-key id: a UUID as 32 lowercase hex chars (the embedded, non-secret part of the bearer).

patch.description: Update an agent key's grants and/or name. Updating grants changes resource/action access without re-issuing the secret. Unknown grant resources return 400.

parameters.name: keyId
parameters.description: Agent-key id: a UUID as 32 lowercase hex chars (the embedded, non-secret part of the bearer).

schema.description: Agent-key id: a UUID as 32 lowercase hex chars (the embedded, non-secret part of the bearer).

post.description:
Rotate an agent key, make-before-break: mints a brand-new key (new id + secret, same
name/scope) and winds the old one down over a grace window (default 3600 s) during which
both tokens authenticate. The old key's id is recorded on the new key as rotatedFrom.

Action.description: An action verb a grant authorizes. The data plane maps each endpoint to one of these.

keyId.description: Agent-key id: a UUID as 32 lowercase hex chars (the embedded, non-secret part of the bearer).

expiresAt.description: Unix seconds after which the key no longer authenticates; 0 = never. > 0 flags a key winding down after rotation.

rotatedFrom.description: The key id this key replaced, when minted by rotation.

AuthenticationErrorResponseContent.description: Authentication credentials are missing, malformed, or invalid.

title.description:
A short, human-readable summary of the problem
                                      type.  It SHOULD NOT change from occurrence to occurrence of the
                                      problem, except for purposes of localization (e.g., using
                                      proactive content negotiation; see [RFC7231], Section 3.4).

status.description: The HTTP status code ([RFC7231], Section 6) generated by the origin server for this occurrence of the problem.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.  It may or may not yield further information if dereferenced.

AuthenticationErrorType.description: Problem type URI for authentication errors.

BadRequestErrorResponseContent.description: Request validation or input decoding failed.

BadRequestErrorType.description: Problem type URI for bad request errors.

customMemoryTypes.description:
Custom memory types to register on the store. Optional; when omitted the
store uses only built-in memory types.

storeId.description: Store identifier (1-64 chars, alphanumeric and dashes). Generated IDs are 32-char UUIDs without dashes.

CustomExtractionStrategy.description: Extraction strategy for a custom memory type.

prompt.description: Prompt used to extract memories of this type from session events.

enabled.description: Whether the strategy is active. Defaults to true when omitted.

CustomField.description: A structured field within a custom memory type.

name.description: Field name. Letters, digits, underscores, and dashes; must start with a letter.

description.description: Human-readable description of the field (1-200 chars).

CustomFieldType.description:
Supported custom field data types. Mirrors the Context Engine
FieldDescription.type vocabulary exactly.

CustomMemoryType.description:
A custom memory type registered on a store: a named category of long-term
memory with its structured fields and, optionally, an extraction strategy.

name.description:
Unique type name within the store. Must not collide with built-in types
(semantic, episodic, message, session_summary_view).

description.description: Human-readable description of what this memory type captures (1-200 chars).

fields.description:
Structured fields that make up a record of this type. Optional; a type
with no fields accepts direct writes carrying no structured attributes.

ExtractionCadenceConfig.description: Cadence configuration for the extraction pipeline.

activeIntervalSeconds.description: Interval in seconds between extraction runs while a session is active (60-600).

FailedDependencyErrorResponseContent.description: A dependent resource required to process the request is unavailable or unhealthy.

                                      type.  It SHOULD NOT change from occurrence to occurrence of the

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.  It may or may not yield further information if dereferenced.

FailedDependencyErrorType.description: Problem type URI for failed-dependency errors.

FieldError.description: Validation error details for a single request field.

field.description: Name of the invalid request field.

ForbiddenErrorResponseContent.description: The caller is authenticated but not allowed to access the requested resource.

storeId.description: Store identifier (1-64 chars, alphanumeric and dashes). Generated IDs are 32-char UUIDs without dashes.

customMemoryTypes.description: Custom memory types registered on the store.

Grant.description: A grant pairs one resource with the actions allowed on it. Use `mem-store` for `resourceType` and `read`, `write`, or both for actions.

resourceId.description: Resource identifier within its type (e.g. a store id).

MemoryTypeStrategyUpdate.description:
A strategy edit targeting one existing custom memory type. Omitted optional
fields are left unchanged.

typeName.description: Name of the existing custom memory type whose strategy is being edited.

prompt.description: Replacement extraction prompt. Omit to leave the prompt unchanged.

enabled.description: Replacement enabled flag. Omit to leave the flag unchanged.

keyId.description: Agent-key id: a UUID as 32 lowercase hex chars (the embedded, non-secret part of the bearer).

token.description:
Agent-key credential returned when the key is minted or rotated. Store it immediately; it is not returned by list or get operations.

Treat the credential as opaque. Present it on each Data Plane request with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, or use a custom header such as `X-Api-Key` when the standard Authorization header is reserved for another credential.

NotFoundErrorResponseContent.description: The requested resource does not exist.

NotFoundErrorType.description: Problem type URI for not-found errors.

ResourceType.description: Resource type a grant authorizes. Product-qualified so values stay unique across IRIS products.

graceSeconds.description: Grace window in seconds the old key keeps authenticating (min 1; default 3600).

keyId.description: Agent-key id: a UUID as 32 lowercase hex chars (the embedded, non-secret part of the bearer).

token.description:
Agent-key credential returned when the key is minted or rotated. Store it immediately; it is not returned by list or get operations.

Treat the credential as opaque. Present it on each Data Plane request with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, or use a custom header such as `X-Api-Key` when the standard Authorization header is reserved for another credential.

oldExpiresAt.description: Unix seconds at which the rotated (old) key stops authenticating.

storeId.description: Store identifier (1-64 chars, alphanumeric and dashes). Generated IDs are 32-char UUIDs without dashes.

customMemoryTypes.description: Custom memory types registered on the store.

SummarizationConfig.description:
Configuration for session summarization.
When enabled, long sessions are periodically condensed into a running summary
while retaining the most recent events.

enabled.description: Whether summarization is enabled for this store

threshold.description: The threshold value that triggers summarization

retainCount.description: Number of most recent events to retain after each summarization cycle.

SummarizationTriggerStrategy.description:
Strategy that determines when summarization is triggered.
Additional strategies such as token_count can be added later.

UnexpectedErrorResponseContent.description: The service failed with an unexpected internal error.

addCustomMemoryTypes.description:
New custom memory types to append. Editing an existing type's prompt or
enabled flag is done via updateCustomMemoryTypeStrategies; renaming,
changing fields, or removing a type is not supported.

updateCustomMemoryTypeStrategies.description: Strategy edits (prompt / enabled) to apply to existing custom memory types.

storeId.description: Store identifier (1-64 chars, alphanumeric and dashes). Generated IDs are 32-char UUIDs without dashes.

customMemoryTypes.description: Custom memory types registered on the store.
